results.name: Aberfeldy
results.water_source: Draws from the Pitilie Burn, a small Highland stream running down from the Grampian hills above Aberfeldy in Perthshire. The naturally soft water filtered through heather and ancient rock contributes to Aberfeldy's signature honeyed, waxy, and fruity character.
results.description: Aberfeldy is the spiritual home of Dewar's blended whisky and draws its water from the famous Pitilie Burn, known for deposits of alluvial gold. The distillery produces a honeyed, waxy Highland malt with notes of heather and orange peel, anchoring the Dewar's White Label and 12-year-old blends.
results.meta_description: Aberfeldy — home of Dewar's whisky since 1896. Honeyed, waxy Highland malt from the gold-bearing Pitilie Burn.

results.name: Bunnahabhain
results.water_source: Sources water from the Margadale Spring on Islay's northeast coast, one of the island's least peaty water sources. This unusually mineral-clean water contributes to Bunnahabhain's notably lighter, less smoky Islay profile.
results.description: Bunnahabhain is unique among Islay distilleries for producing predominantly unpeated single malt, with a nutty, sherried, and maritime character. The distillery sits at the head of a bay on Islay's northeast coast, drawing water from the Margadale spring.
results.meta_description: Bunnahabhain — predominantly unpeated Islay single malt since 1881. Nutty, sherried, and coastal character.

results.name: Glenfiddich
results.water_source: Sources water from the Robbie Dhu spring, fed by the Conval Hills above Dufftown in Speyside. This naturally soft, pure water is central to Glenfiddich's clean, approachable single malt character.
results.description: Glenfiddich is the world's best-selling single malt Scotch whisky, pioneering the category's global growth since the 1960s. The family-owned distillery in Dufftown remains one of the few Scottish distilleries with its own on-site cooperage and coppersmith.
results.meta_description: Glenfiddich — world's best-selling single malt since 1887. Family-owned Speyside distillery in Dufftown.

results.name: The Balvenie
results.water_source: Draws from the Robbie Dhu spring in the Conval Hills, the same Speyside source shared with neighboring Glenfiddich. This soft, pure water allows The Balvenie's honeyed, complex character to develop freely.
results.description: The Balvenie is one of the few Scottish distilleries that still maintains its own floor maltings, cooperage, and coppersmith. Located next to Glenfiddich in Dufftown, it is celebrated for its honeyed, rich character and innovative cask-finishing program including DoubleWood and Caribbean Cask.
results.meta_description: The Balvenie — traditional craft with floor maltings and on-site cooperage since 1892. Honeyed Speyside single malts.

results.name: The Glenlivet
results.water_source: Draws from Josie's Well, a natural spring rising from granite hills above Livet Glen in Speyside. The exceptionally pure, mineral-light water contributes to the distillery's signature delicate, floral character.
results.description: The Glenlivet was the first distillery in the parish of Glenlivet to obtain a legal license after the Excise Act of 1823. It established the Speyside style of light, fruity, and elegant single malt and remains one of the top-selling Scotch whiskies worldwide.
results.meta_description: The Glenlivet — first legally licensed Speyside distillery in 1824. Light, fruity, and elegantly defined single malt.

results.name: Clynelish
results.water_source: Draws from the Clynemilton Burn flowing through the hills above Brora in Sutherland. The soft, lightly peated water from this northern Highland source contributes to Clynelish's waxy, coastal complexity.
results.description: Clynelish distillery in Brora produces a distinctively waxy, maritime single malt prized by independent bottlers and blenders alike. Built to replace the original Brora distillery, Clynelish's oily, coastal character with tropical fruit and beeswax notes has earned it a cult following.
results.meta_description: Clynelish — waxy, maritime Highland malt from Brora since 1967. Coastal character with tropical fruit and beeswax.

results.name: Hellyers Road
results.water_source: Draws from underground springs fed by rainfall filtering through the basalt-rich soils of north-western Tasmania near Burnie. The naturally soft, mineral-pure Tasmanian water contributes to Hellyers Road's approachable, creamy, and lightly fruited single malt character.
results.description: Hellyers Road in Burnie is Tasmania's largest whisky distillery, owned by a local dairy co-operative. The distillery produces both peated and unpeated single malts using Tasmanian barley and pure water from the region's ancient rainforests. Its output has grown from craft-scale to one of Australia's most widely available single malts.
results.meta_description: Hellyers Road — Tasmania's largest whisky distillery since 1999. Dairy co-op owned, rainforest water, and wide availability.

results.name: Highland Park
results.water_source: Sources water from Crantit Springs near Kirkwall on Orkney, percolating through Old Red Sandstone. The slightly hard, mineral-balanced water complements Highland Park's unique mix of peat smoke, heather, and honey.
results.description: Highland Park is the northernmost Scotch whisky distillery, located in Kirkwall, Orkney. The distillery hand-turns its own locally cut Orkney peat on traditional floor maltings, creating a heather-honey sweetness layered with gentle smoke that defines the Highland Park style.
results.meta_description: Highland Park — northernmost Scotch distillery in Orkney since 1798. Heather-honey sweetness with gentle peat smoke.

results.name: Oban
results.water_source: Sources water from a spring above the town of Oban on Scotland's west coast, filtered through granite hillside. The pure, moderately soft water helps deliver Oban's signature balance of Highland richness and coastal brine.
results.description: Oban is one of the smallest distilleries in Diageo's Classic Malts range, squeezed between a cliff and the town's main street in the West Highland port town. Its 14 Year Old bridges Highland and Island styles with a lightly smoky, sea-salt character and rich dried fruit notes.
results.meta_description: Oban — intimate West Highland distillery since 1794. Bridging Highland and Island styles in a compact setting.

results.name: Tobermory
results.water_source: Sources water from Aros Loch in the hills above Tobermory on the Isle of Mull. The soft, peaty highland water from this island catchment contributes to the distillery's distinctive, sometimes spicy island character.
results.description: Tobermory is the only distillery on the Isle of Mull, producing two distinct styles: the unpeated Tobermory and the heavily peated Ledaig. Founded in 1798, the distillery has had a turbulent history of closures and revivals but now produces highly regarded single malts in both styles.
results.meta_description: Tobermory — Mull's only distillery since 1798. Unpeated Tobermory and heavily peated Ledaig single malts.

results.name: Dingle
results.water_source: Draws from local springs near the town of Dingle on the windswept Dingle Peninsula in County Kerry. This soft Atlantic-facing water, naturally purified through sandstone, complements the distillery's small-batch, handcrafted Irish whiskey character.
results.description: Dingle Distillery on the Wild Atlantic Way in County Kerry was one of the first wave of Ireland's craft distillery movement. Its small copper pot stills produce single malt and pot still whiskey in limited batches, with each release numbered and highly sought after by collectors.
results.meta_description: Dingle — craft Irish distillery on the Wild Atlantic Way since 2012. Small-batch, numbered releases from County Kerry.

results.name: MGP
results.water_source: Sources water from the Great Miami River aquifer system beneath Lawrenceburg, Indiana, providing exceptionally consistent, soft water. MGP's reliable, low-mineral water supply supports the large-scale, consistent contract distillation operations that supply dozens of American whiskey brands.
results.description: MGP (formerly Seagram's Lawrenceburg distillery) in Indiana is one of America's largest and most prolific whiskey producers, supplying bourbon and rye to over 50 brands. The distillery operates massive column and pot stills and is particularly renowned for its high-rye bourbon and 95% rye whiskey recipes.
results.meta_description: MGP — America's largest whiskey source distillery since 1847. Supplying 50+ brands with bourbon and rye from Lawrenceburg, Indiana.

results.name: Michter's
results.water_source: Draws from a dedicated limestone spring aquifer at its Shively, Kentucky distillery, treating the water to precise mineral specifications. This carefully managed water source supports Michter's commitment to small-batch quality across its bourbon and rye expressions.
results.description: Michter's traces its heritage to America's first whiskey company, dating to 1753 in Pennsylvania. Revived in Kentucky in the 2000s, Michter's focuses on small-batch and single-barrel expressions with toasted barrel finishes and a commitment to quality over volume.
results.meta_description: Michter's — heritage dating to 1753, revived in Kentucky. Small-batch bourbon and rye with toasted barrel finishes.

results.name: Stranahan's
results.water_source: Draws from Denver's municipal supply sourced from Rocky Mountain snowmelt in the South Platte River watershed. This exceptionally pure, mineral-light alpine water suits Stranahan's approachable, lightly sweet Colorado single malt style.
results.description: Stranahan's in Denver, Colorado was one of the first American single malt whiskey producers. Made from 100% malted barley and aged in new charred American oak barrels, it benefits from Colorado's dramatic temperature swings for accelerated maturation at altitude.
results.meta_description: Stranahan's — Colorado American single malt since 2004. High-altitude aging with dramatic temperature swings in Denver.

results.name: Miyagikyo
results.water_source: Sources water from the confluence of the Hirose and Nikkawa rivers in Sendai, Miyagi prefecture. The soft, pure river water from this verdant valley setting contributes to Miyagikyo's characteristically elegant, floral, and fruity style.
results.description: Miyagikyo is Nikka's second distillery, located in a misty valley in Miyagi Prefecture. Masataka Taketsuru chose the site for its humid, foggy microclimate. The distillery produces an elegant, fruity, and delicate style that complements Yoichi's boldness in Nikka's blends.
results.meta_description: Miyagikyo — Nikka's elegant Honshu distillery since 1969. Fruity, delicate whisky from a misty Miyagi valley.

results.name: Dalwhinnie
results.water_source: Sources water from the Allt an Doire Mhuir burn near Drumochter Pass in the Grampian Highlands, at an altitude of 326 meters. The exceptionally soft, snowmelt-fed water — among the purest in Scotland — gives Dalwhinnie its light, heathery, and honeyed Highland style.
results.description: Dalwhinnie is the highest distillery in Scotland at 326 meters above sea level, situated at the gateway to the Highlands. Its extreme location creates a slow, gentle maturation that produces a delicate, honeyed malt. Selected as the Highland representative in Diageo's Classic Malts of Scotland.
results.meta_description: Dalwhinnie — Scotland's highest distillery at 326m since 1897. Delicate, honeyed Highland malt from the Classic Malts series.

results.name: Auchentoshan
results.water_source: Sources water from Cochno Loch in the Kilpatrick Hills above Dalmuir, on the outskirts of Glasgow in the Scottish Lowlands. The very soft, lightly mineral Lowland water suits Auchentoshan's uniquely triple-distilled light and clean style.
results.description: Auchentoshan is the only Scottish distillery to fully triple-distill every drop of spirit, following the traditional Lowland practice. Located on the outskirts of Glasgow, this produces an exceptionally smooth, light, and delicate malt that serves as an accessible entry point to single malt Scotch.
results.meta_description: Auchentoshan — Scotland's only fully triple-distilled single malt since 1823. Smooth and delicate Lowland whisky near Glasgow.

results.name: Cardhu
results.water_source: Sources water from the Mannoch Hill springs above Knockando in Speyside, which have supplied the distillery since 1884. The naturally soft, clean hill spring water forms the backbone of Cardhu's smooth, light, and accessible Speyside character.
results.description: Cardhu holds a special place in whisky history as a distillery pioneered by Helen Cumming and her daughter-in-law Elizabeth, among the first women to run a Scottish distillery. It is the spiritual home of Johnnie Walker, providing the key malt component for the world's best-selling blended Scotch.
results.meta_description: Cardhu — spiritual home of Johnnie Walker since 1824. Pioneering women-led distillery with sweet, honeyed Speyside malt.

results.name: Glenfarclas
results.water_source: Sources water from the Green Spring at the foot of Ben Rinnes mountain in Speyside, considered one of Scotland's finest distillery water sources. The naturally soft, crystal-clear spring water contributes to Glenfarclas's full, rich, and sherried Highland character.
results.description: Glenfarclas has been owned by six generations of the Grant family, making it one of the few truly independent Speyside distilleries. Known for its direct-fired stills and exclusive use of sherry casks, Glenfarclas produces rich, full-bodied malts with its legendary Family Casks series spanning vintages from the 1950s onward.
results.meta_description: Glenfarclas — six-generation family-owned Speyside distillery since 1836. Sherry-cask mastery and legendary Family Casks.

results.name: Glen Grant
results.water_source: Sources water from the Black Burn, a hillside stream that flows through heather moorland above Rothes in Speyside. The clean, naturally soft water contributes to Glen Grant's light, clean, and fruity signature single malt style.
results.description: Glen Grant is one of the best-selling single malts in the world, particularly dominant in the Italian market. Founded by brothers John and James Grant in Rothes, the distillery is known for its tall, slender stills with purifiers that produce a light, crisp, and apple-forward spirit.
results.meta_description: Glen Grant — globally popular Speyside single malt since 1840. Light, crisp spirit from tall stills with purifiers.

results.name: Scapa
results.water_source: Draws from the Lingro Burn on the Orkney Mainland, fed by springs filtering through Old Red Sandstone. The moderately hard, mineral-rich Orkney water gives Scapa its distinctive floral, heathery, and slightly waxy character.
results.description: Scapa is Orkney's other distillery alongside Highland Park, overlooking the famous Scapa Flow naval anchorage. It is one of the few Scottish distilleries to use a Lomond still — a hybrid design — which creates a smooth, honeyed, and subtly briny character distinct from its neighbor.
results.meta_description: Scapa — Orkney distillery overlooking Scapa Flow since 1885. Smooth, honeyed malt from a rare Lomond still.

results.name: The Dalmore
results.water_source: Sources water from the Alness River and local Highland springs filtered through ancient sandstone. The naturally hard, mineral-laden water contributes to The Dalmore's notably rich and robust Highland style.
results.description: The Dalmore is distinguished by its signature stag emblem and a commitment to exceptional sherry-cask finishing. Master blender Richard Paterson has guided the distillery for over 50 years, creating some of the world's most expensive single malt releases.
results.meta_description: The Dalmore — iconic stag emblem and sherry-cask mastery since 1839. Luxury Highland single malts.

results.name: Glenkinchie
results.water_source: Sources water from the Kinchie Burn in East Lothian, a Lowland stream flowing through the agricultural heartland south of Edinburgh. The soft, slightly mineral water suits Glenkinchie's distinctively light, grassy, and floral Lowland style.
results.description: Glenkinchie is known as 'The Edinburgh Malt,' situated just 15 miles from the Scottish capital in the rolling farmland of East Lothian. Selected as the Lowland representative in Diageo's Classic Malts, it produces a light, grassy, and floral spirit with one of the largest wash stills in Scotland.
results.meta_description: Glenkinchie — 'The Edinburgh Malt' since 1837. Diageo Classic Malts Lowland representative, light and grassy.

results.name: West Cork
results.water_source: Sources water from local springs and streams in the rugged hills of West Cork, naturally filtered through the region's sandstone and shale geology. The naturally soft Atlantic-facing water suits West Cork's range of approachable Irish whiskey styles.
results.description: West Cork Distillers is one of Ireland's largest independent distilleries, founded by three friends in a small town in County Cork. Using Atlantic maritime air for maturation and producing a range of blended, single malt, and single pot still whiskeys, it has become one of Ireland's fastest-growing whiskey brands.
results.meta_description: West Cork — independent Irish distillery since 2003. Atlantic maritime maturation and rapid growth from County Cork.

results.name: Castle & Key
results.water_source: Draws from natural limestone springs on the historic Old Taylor distillery grounds in Millville, Kentucky, the same source once used by Colonel E.H. Taylor himself. The naturally iron-free, mineral-rich limestone spring water is integral to Castle & Key's revival of this historic site.
results.description: Castle & Key occupies the beautifully restored Old Taylor distillery in Millville, Kentucky, a National Historic Landmark. The distillery focuses on small-batch bourbon and rye using heritage grain varieties and natural spring water. Its sunken gardens and castle-like architecture make it one of the most photogenic distilleries in America.
results.meta_description: Castle & Key — restored Old Taylor distillery and National Historic Landmark. Heritage grain bourbon from Millville, Kentucky.

results.name: Canadian Club (Hiram Walker)
results.water_source: Sources water from the Detroit River and Lake St. Clair system in Windsor, Ontario, drawing on the Great Lakes freshwater basin. The naturally soft Great Lakes water, treated to brewing quality, supports Canadian Club's smooth, light, and blended Canadian whisky character.
results.description: Canadian Club is produced at the historic Hiram Walker distillery in Windsor, Ontario, one of the largest distilleries in North America. Founded in 1858, the brand pioneered the practice of blending whisky before aging (pre-blending) and was a favorite spirit smuggled across the Detroit River during Prohibition.
results.meta_description: Canadian Club — historic Windsor distillery since 1858. Pre-blended Canadian whisky and Prohibition-era legend.

results.name: Eigashima (White Oak)
results.water_source: Sources water from underground springs near Akashi Bay in Hyogo prefecture, an area historically known for clean coastal water. The moderately mineral spring water, tempered by maritime influence, contributes to Eigashima's light and delicately crafted Akashi whisky style.
results.description: Eigashima Shuzo in Akashi, Hyogo Prefecture holds Japan's first whisky license, granted in 1919 — four years before Yamazaki. The distillery primarily produces sake and shochu, with whisky made only a few months per year. Its Akashi single malt and blends are valued for their coastal, approachable character.
results.meta_description: Eigashima — holder of Japan's first whisky license since 1919. Coastal Akashi whiskies from sake-and-shochu maker in Hyogo.

results.name: The Macallan
results.water_source: Draws from Easter Elchies spring water, naturally filtered through red granite on Speyside. The mineral-rich, soft water contributes to The Macallan's characteristically full-bodied and rich spirit.
results.description: The Macallan is one of the world's most collectible and prestigious single malt brands, renowned for its sherry-cask maturation program. Located on the Easter Elchies estate above the River Spey, the distillery opened a striking Norman Foster-designed facility in 2018.
results.meta_description: The Macallan — iconic Speyside distillery since 1824. Sherry-cask single malts and a world-class visitor center.

results.name: Aberlour
results.water_source: Sources water from St. Drostan's Well, a natural spring in the foothills above Aberlour on Speyside. The soft, iron-free water is credited with the distillery's smooth, richly fruited spirit profile.
results.description: Aberlour distillery draws water from a natural spring in the heart of Speyside and is known for its rich, sherried style. The A'bunadh cask-strength range is particularly celebrated among whisky enthusiasts for its intense sherry character and non-chill-filtered presentation.
results.meta_description: Aberlour — rich, sherried Speyside single malts since 1879. Home of the acclaimed A'bunadh cask-strength range.

results.name: GlenDronach
results.water_source: Draws from the Dronach Burn, a highland stream in Aberdeenshire filtered through heather moorland and granite rock. The soft, lightly peated water lends GlenDronach its distinctive earthy depth and smooth texture.
results.description: GlenDronach is celebrated for its dedication to sherry-cask maturation, using both Oloroso and Pedro Ximenez casks from Jerez. Restarted in 2002 after a silent period, the distillery has built a devoted following for its richly sherried, traditional Highland-Speyside character.
results.meta_description: GlenDronach — sherry-cask specialists since 1826. Richly matured in Oloroso and Pedro Ximenez casks.

results.name: Bushmills
results.water_source: Draws from St. Columb's Rill, a natural stream whose waters rise in the basalt hills above Bushmills in County Antrim. This clean, soft water — having filtered through ancient volcanic basalt — contributes to Bushmills' famously light and approachable Irish whiskey.
results.description: Bushmills holds the oldest licensed distillery claim in the world, with a grant to distill dating to 1608 in County Antrim, Northern Ireland. The distillery triple-distills its single malts and uses a combination of bourbon and sherry casks for a smooth, honeyed character.
results.meta_description: Bushmills — world's oldest licensed distillery since 1608. Triple-distilled Irish single malt from County Antrim.

results.name: Amrut
results.water_source: Sources water from deep bore wells drawing on underground reserves in Bangalore, Karnataka, filtered through the Deccan Plateau's ancient granite. The very soft, mineral-clean southern Indian water supports Amrut's rapid tropical maturation and bold, spice-forward character.
results.description: Amrut in Bangalore was the first Indian distillery to release a single malt whisky for international markets in 2004. The high altitude and tropical heat of Bangalore produce an angel's share of 10-12% annually, resulting in intensely concentrated and richly flavored single malts.
results.meta_description: Amrut — India's single malt pioneer since 1948. Bangalore's tropical climate producing intensely concentrated whisky.

results.name: Angel's Envy
results.water_source: Sources water from a limestone-filtered deep aquifer beneath Louisville, Kentucky, treating it to remove iron common in the region. This carefully prepared limestone-character water supports Angel's Envy's smooth, port-cask-finished bourbon production.
results.description: Angel's Envy was founded by master distiller Lincoln Henderson, who had previously created Woodford Reserve. The brand pioneered the premium finished bourbon category, with its flagship bourbon finished in port wine barrels from Portugal. The distillery in downtown Louisville has become a major Bourbon Trail destination.
results.meta_description: Angel's Envy — port-barrel-finished bourbon since 2010. Lincoln Henderson's legacy in downtown Louisville.

results.name: Bowmore
results.water_source: Draws from the Laggan River on Islay, flowing through peat moorland before collection. The softly peated water gives Bowmore its balanced character — sitting between Islay's most intense smoky expressions and lighter coastal styles.
results.description: Bowmore is the oldest licensed distillery on Islay and one of the oldest in Scotland. Situated on the shores of Loch Indaal, it produces a medium-peated style that bridges the gap between heavily peated south coast and lighter north coast Islay malts.
results.meta_description: Bowmore — Islay's oldest distillery since 1779. Medium-peated single malts with maritime influence from Loch Indaal.

results.name: Buffalo Trace
results.water_source: Uses limestone-filtered water from the Kentucky River and local deep aquifers in Frankfort, Kentucky. The naturally calcium- and magnesium-rich water is ideal for bourbon fermentation, contributing to Buffalo Trace's smooth, full-flavored profile.
results.description: Buffalo Trace is one of America's oldest continuously operating distilleries, producing a vast portfolio that includes Pappy Van Winkle, Eagle Rare, Blanton's, and its eponymous bourbon. The distillery is a National Historic Landmark and has won more awards than any other North American distillery.
results.meta_description: Buffalo Trace — America's most awarded distillery since 1773. Home of Pappy Van Winkle, Blanton's, and Eagle Rare.

results.name: GlenAllachie
results.water_source: Sources water from the River Allachie, a clean Speyside tributary flowing through heather-covered hills. The soft, low-mineral water suits GlenAllachie's style of rich, layered, slowly matured single malts.
results.description: GlenAllachie was acquired in 2017 by former BenRiach master distiller Billy Walker, who transformed it from a blending workhorse into a celebrated single malt producer. The distillery holds one of the largest cask inventories in Speyside, with an emphasis on sherry-cask maturation and cask-strength releases.
results.meta_description: GlenAllachie — Speyside distillery reborn under Billy Walker since 2017. Sherry-cask focus and acclaimed cask-strength releases.

results.name: Crown Royal
results.water_source: Sources water from Gimli, Manitoba on the western shore of Lake Winnipeg, drawing from the lake's vast freshwater reserves. The naturally pure, exceptionally soft glacial lake water contributes to Crown Royal's smooth, gentle Canadian whisky character.
results.description: Crown Royal was created in 1939 to commemorate the visit of King George VI and Queen Elizabeth to Canada. Produced at the Gimli distillery in Manitoba on the shores of Lake Winnipeg, Crown Royal blends over 50 distinct whiskies to achieve its signature smooth, slightly sweet character in a distinctive purple bag.
results.meta_description: Crown Royal — Canada's iconic whisky since 1939. Over 50 whiskies blended in Gimli, Manitoba for smooth, regal character.

results.name: Four Roses
results.water_source: Sources water from a limestone-filtered spring aquifer beneath the distillery in Lawrenceburg, Kentucky. This naturally soft, iron-free water is particularly prized for bourbon production and contributes to Four Roses' delicate, floral character.
results.description: Four Roses is unique among Kentucky distillers in using 10 distinct bourbon recipes — two mashbills combined with five proprietary yeast strains. This approach allows exceptional blending complexity. The distillery's Spanish Mission-style architecture in Lawrenceburg is a Kentucky landmark.
results.meta_description: Four Roses — 10 unique bourbon recipes from two mashbills and five yeasts since 1888. Spanish Mission-style landmark.

results.name: Glen Scotia
results.water_source: Draws from the Cross Water of Loch in Campbeltown, a coastal burgh on the Kintyre Peninsula. The soft, lightly mineral water contributes to Glen Scotia's characteristic briny, coastal Campbeltown profile.
results.description: Glen Scotia is one of only three remaining distilleries in Campbeltown, once Scotland's whisky capital with over 30 distilleries. Revived under the Loch Lomond Group, Glen Scotia produces a maritime, briny style with gentle peat smoke that captures the essence of the Campbeltown region.
results.meta_description: Glen Scotia — Campbeltown's maritime malt since 1832. Briny, gently smoky, and revived in Scotland's whisky capital.

results.name: Heaven Hill
results.water_source: Sources water from limestone-filtered springs and deep aquifers beneath the Bardstown, Kentucky area. The naturally calcium-rich, iron-free water is integral to Heaven Hill's range of distinctly Kentucky-character bourbons and ryes.
results.description: Heaven Hill is the largest independent, family-owned distillery in the United States, producing brands including Elijah Craig, Evan Williams, Larceny, and Henry McKenna. The Shapira family has owned the company since its founding, making it one of Kentucky's most significant bourbon producers.
results.meta_description: Heaven Hill — largest independent family-owned US distillery since 1935. Home of Elijah Craig and Evan Williams.

results.name: Hudson
results.water_source: Sources water from local wells drawing on the Catskill Mountains aquifer system in Gardiner, New York. The naturally soft Catskill mountain water supports Hudson's farm-to-bottle approach and contributes to the approachable character of its New York-grown grain whiskies.
results.description: Hudson Whiskey from Tuthilltown Spirits in the Hudson Valley was one of the first craft whiskeys to challenge the Kentucky establishment. Its Baby Bourbon — aged in small barrels for accelerated maturation — helped launch the American craft distilling revolution. The distillery now produces a full range of bourbon, rye, and single malt.
results.meta_description: Hudson — craft whiskey pioneer from the Hudson Valley since 2003. Small-barrel aging and the spark of the craft revolution.

results.name: Jack Daniel's
results.water_source: Sources water from the Cave Spring Hollow on the distillery property in Lynchburg, Tennessee, naturally filtered through iron-rich limestone. The iron-bearing water is removed through a charcoal filtration process before use, yielding the clean base for Jack Daniel's smooth Tennessee whiskey.
results.description: Jack Daniel's is the world's best-selling American whiskey, produced in Lynchburg, Tennessee since 1866. Every drop is charcoal-mellowed through 10 feet of sugar maple charcoal before aging, a process that gives Tennessee whiskey its distinctive smooth character.
results.meta_description: Jack Daniel's — world's best-selling American whiskey since 1866. Charcoal-mellowed Tennessee whiskey from Lynchburg.

results.name: Midleton
results.water_source: Sources water from local springs in County Cork, Ireland, naturally filtered through the limestone-rich geology of the Midleton area. This soft, clean Irish water is central to the exceptionally smooth and approachable character of all spirits made at Midleton.
results.description: Midleton Distillery in Cork is Ireland's largest, producing Jameson, Redbreast, Green Spot, Yellow Spot, Powers, and Midleton Very Rare under one roof. The modern distillery replaced the original 1825 facility and houses both pot stills and column stills for diverse production.
results.meta_description: Midleton — Ireland's largest distillery producing Jameson, Redbreast, and Midleton Very Rare since 1975.

results.name: Teeling
results.water_source: Sources water from the Dublin municipal supply drawn from the Wicklow Mountains reservoir system. Teeling treats this soft, granite-filtered Irish water to precise brewing specifications, supporting the distillery's light and fruit-forward urban Dublin whiskey style.
results.description: Teeling was the first new distillery in Dublin in over 125 years when it opened in the Liberties neighborhood in 2015. Founded by Jack and Stephen Teeling, it has revitalized Dublin's distilling heritage with innovative cask finishes and non-chill-filtered releases.
results.meta_description: Teeling — first new Dublin distillery in 125 years, opened 2015. Innovative cask finishes in the Liberties.

results.name: Waterford
results.water_source: Draws from the River Suir near Waterford city in the southeast of Ireland, a region with rich agricultural heritage and pure river water. The soft Irish river water supports Waterford's terroir-focused single farm origin approach to Irish whisky production.
results.description: Waterford Distillery was founded by Mark Reynier, the former owner of Bruichladdich, with a mission to prove terroir in whisky. The distillery tracks every barley farm individually, producing single-farm-origin single malts that demonstrate how soil, climate, and variety influence whiskey flavor.
results.meta_description: Waterford — terroir-driven Irish single malt since 2015. Single-farm-origin barley proving terroir in whiskey.

results.name: Yamazaki
results.water_source: Draws from the confluence of the Katsura River and Uji River in Osaka prefecture, an area long celebrated for its pure water. The naturally soft water is a key reason Suntory chose this site, and it remains fundamental to Yamazaki's elegant, delicate spirit.
results.description: Yamazaki is Japan's first and oldest whisky distillery, founded by Shinjiro Torii in 1923 at the confluence of three rivers near Kyoto. The distillery uses diverse still shapes and cask types including mizunara oak to create a wide range of flavor profiles for Suntory's blending program.
results.meta_description: Yamazaki — Japan's first whisky distillery since 1923. Diverse still shapes, mizunara oak, and Suntory heritage near Kyoto.

results.name: Wild Turkey
results.water_source: Sources water from a deep limestone aquifer beneath the distillery site in Lawrenceburg, Kentucky. The naturally iron-free, calcium-rich limestone water supports robust fermentation and contributes to Wild Turkey's bold, full-bodied bourbon character.
results.description: Wild Turkey is known for producing bold, full-flavored bourbon under the guidance of master distiller Jimmy Russell, who has worked at the distillery since 1954. The distillery uses a lower entry proof into the barrel than most competitors, resulting in more flavor extraction from the wood.
results.meta_description: Wild Turkey — bold Kentucky bourbon since 1869. Master distiller Jimmy Russell's lower entry proof for richer flavor.

results.name: Woodford Reserve
results.water_source: Draws from Glenn's Creek, a pure local waterway fed by springs filtered through Kentucky's limestone bedrock near Versailles. The mineral-balanced limestone water is central to Woodford Reserve's smooth, complex flavor and long finish.
results.description: Woodford Reserve operates from one of the oldest and smallest distilleries in Kentucky, using copper pot stills and triple distillation — unique among major bourbon producers. The distillery in Versailles produces a refined, complex bourbon with over 200 detectable flavor notes.
results.meta_description: Woodford Reserve — Kentucky's pot-distilled bourbon since 1812. Triple distillation for refined complexity.

results.name: Glenmorangie
results.water_source: Draws from the Tarlogie Springs on the hills above Tain in the Northern Highlands, one of Scotland's most celebrated distillery water sources. This exceptionally pure, mineral-light spring water enables Glenmorangie's famously light and floral character.
results.description: Glenmorangie operates the tallest pot stills in Scotland, standing over 5 meters high, which produce an exceptionally light and elegant spirit. The distillery pioneered wood-finishing in the whisky industry and its core range includes finishes in Sauternes, sherry, and Burgundy casks.
results.meta_description: Glenmorangie — tallest stills in Scotland producing elegant Highland malt since 1843. Pioneers of cask finishing.

results.name: Lagavulin
results.water_source: Sources water from the Solan Lochs on Islay's southern coast, naturally filtered through peat bogs and ancient rock. The soft, peat-tinged water reinforces Lagavulin's powerfully smoky and maritime character.
results.description: Lagavulin is one of Islay's most revered distilleries, producing intensely peated and deeply complex single malts. Its 16 Year Old is considered a benchmark for the Islay style, combining powerful smoke with dried fruit sweetness and a long maritime finish.
results.meta_description: Lagavulin — benchmark Islay single malt since 1816. Intensely peated with deep complexity and maritime character.
